model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: This is an exceptionally close matchup between two elite defensive teams, with Virginia (KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49) and Tennessee (KenPom #15, AdjEM +26.06) separated by less than half a point in adjusted efficiency margin and virtually identical defensive ratings (AdjD 95.7 vs 95.0). Virginia's decisive edge comes from its 29-4 record and 15-3 ACC mark as the conference runner-up, reflecting far greater consistency than Tennessee's 22-11 record that included an early SEC Tournament exit to Vanderbilt, while the Cavaliers' slight offensive advantage (AdjO 122.2 vs 121.1) could be the difference in what projects as a low-scoring, grind-it-out affair. Tennessee's tougher schedule (SOS 18th vs 63rd) and the return of Nate Ament from injury give the Vols a credible path, but Virginia's superior record, higher seed, and momentum from a dominant ACC season make them the narrow favorite.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest Round of 32: Tennessee vs Virginia
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia edges Tennessee in a near coin-flip matchup between two elite defensive teams with nearly identical efficiency profiles, with Virginia's far superior 29-4 record and slight offensive edge tipping the balance.
evidence: KenPom rankings: Virginia #13 (AdjEM +26.49, AdjO 122.2, AdjD 95.7) vs Tennessee #15 (AdjEM +26.06, AdjO 121.1, AdjD 95.0)
Torvik rankings: Virginia #14 vs Tennessee #15 with nearly identical adjusted offensive and defensive efficiencies
Virginia 29-4 (15-3 ACC, 2nd place) vs Tennessee 22-11 (11-7 SEC, 4th place)
SRS: Tennessee 22.08 (17th) vs Virginia 21.59 (19th); SOS: Tennessee 11.99 (18th) vs Virginia 9.36 (63rd)
Tennessee's Nate Ament returning from late-season leg injury with a week of rest before tournament
Tennessee lost to Vanderbilt 75-68 in SEC Tournament; Virginia was ACC runner-up with dominant regular season

result.text: Tennessee basketball needed rest for Nate Ament, Ja'Kobi Gillespie for March Madness Skip to main content Home Sports GoVolsXtra PrepXtra Local Sports Sports Columnists Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Rick Barnes on Tennessee, Nate Ament's health, Miami (Ohio) for March Madness Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Tennessee basketball lost early in the SEC Tournament, but it left Nashville with quite the consolation prize. Rest. The Vols (22-11) earned a No. 6 seed in the 2026 NCAA Tournament, their lowest seeding under coach Rick Barnes. More important than seeding, though, is the week-long break between Tennessee's 75-68 loss to Vanderbilt on March 13 and the Vols ' first-round game at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia on March 20 (4:25 p.m. ET, TBS). Tennessee awaits the winner of a First Four game between No. 11 Miami of Ohio (31-1) and No. 11 SMU (20-13), who play at UD Arena in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18 (9:15 p.m. ET, TruTV). Offensive stars Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie benefit most from the days off of postseason basketball. Ament barely finished a single practice before jumping into consecutive SEC Tournament games against Auburn and Vanderbilt. The 6-foot-10 freshman dazzled in his first game back from a right ankle sprain injury that forced him to miss the last two weeks of the regular season. He had 27 points and eight rebounds in the Vols' 72-62 win over Auburn, but fizzled out with a 1-of-13 shooting performance against the Commodores on March 13. "I think it was good for him to get some of the rust off," said Barnes on March 15. "I thought it was important that he did get to play in Nashville. I'm not sure that the doctors wanted him to play as much as he played. I'm not sure if we kept winning how much he would've played. "If it were up to him, well, you'd have to sit him down, because he wanted to play." Ament, who hadn't played since Feb. 28 prior to the SEC Tournament, refused to pin exhaustion for the poor shooting. "Yeah, there's definitely some fatigue, but I'm not going to blame it one fatigue why I didn't make shots," he said after the loss. "I just have to be better." Tennessee's leading scorer Ja'Kobi Gillespie could also use the rest. He and Jaylen Carey are the only Vols who have played in all 33 games. The All-SEC guard leads Tennessee with 1,137 total minutes and still averaged 18 points, 5.5 assists, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 steals per game. "A little more time will certainly benefit (Ament), and maybe Ja'Kobi more than anybody," Barnes said. "Ja'Kobi's played a lot of minutes." Rick Barnes agrees with Miami (Ohio) making 2026 NCAA Tournament Miami (Ohio) qualified for the 2026 NCAA Tournament after going an undefeated 31-0 in the regular season. The RedHawks didn't have any wins over power conference opponents, and lost in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals to UMass. Miami (Ohio) still made the tournament as a No. 11 seed and will play No. 11 SMU to get into the Round of 64 against Tennessee. Barnes was impresse

result.text: Tennessee basketball needed rest for Nate Ament, Ja'Kobi Gillespie for March Madness Skip to main content Home Sports GoVolsXtra PrepXtra Local Sports Sports Columnists Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Rick Barnes on Tennessee, Nate Ament's health, Miami (Ohio) for March Madness Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Tennessee basketball lost early in the SEC Tournament, but it left Nashville with quite the consolation prize. Rest. The Vols (22-11) earned a No. 6 seed in the 2026 NCAA Tournament, their lowest seeding under coach Rick Barnes. More important than seeding, though, is the week-long break between Tennessee's 75-68 loss to Vanderbilt on March 13 and the Vols ' first-round game at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia on March 20 (4:25 p.m. ET, TBS). Tennessee awaits the winner of a First Four game between No. 11 Miami of Ohio (31-1) and No. 11 SMU (20-13), who play at UD Arena in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18 (9:15 p.m. ET, TruTV). Offensive stars Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie benefit most from the days off of postseason basketball. Ament barely finished a single practice before jumping into consecutive SEC Tournament games against Auburn and Vanderbilt. The 6-foot-10 freshman dazzled in his first game back from a right ankle sprain injury that forced him to miss the last two weeks of the regular season. He had 27 points and eight rebounds in the Vols' 72-62 win over Auburn, but fizzled out with a 1-of-13 shooting performance against the Commodores on March 13. "I think it was good for him to get some of the rust off," said Barnes on March 15. "I thought it was important that he did get to play in Nashville. I'm not sure that the doctors wanted him to play as much as he played. I'm not sure if we kept winning how much he would've played. "If it were up to him, well, you'd have to sit him down, because he wanted to play." Ament, who hadn't played since Feb. 28 prior to the SEC Tournament, refused to pin exhaustion for the poor shooting. "Yeah, there's definitely some fatigue, but I'm not going to blame it one fatigue why I didn't make shots," he said after the loss. "I just have to be better." Tennessee's leading scorer Ja'Kobi Gillespie could also use the rest. He and Jaylen Carey are the only Vols who have played in all 33 games. The All-SEC guard leads Tennessee with 1,137 total minutes and still averaged 18 points, 5.5 assists, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 steals per game. "A little more time will certainly benefit (Ament), and maybe Ja'Kobi more than anybody," Barnes said. "Ja'Kobi's played a lot of minutes." Rick Barnes agrees with Miami (Ohio) making 2026 NCAA Tournament Miami (Ohio) qualified for the 2026 NCAA Tournament after going an undefeated 31-0 in the regular season. The RedHawks didn't have any wins over power conference opponents, and lost in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals to UMass. Miami (Ohio) still made the tournament as a No. 11 seed and will play No. 11 SMU to get into the Round of 64 against Tennessee. Barnes was impresse

modelTrace.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me analyze the key data:

**Virginia (3-seed):**
- Record: 29-4 (15-3 ACC, 2nd)
- KenPom: #13, AdjEM +26.49, AdjO 122.2 (28th), AdjD 95.7
- Torvik: #14, AdjOE 121.75 (31st), AdjDE 95.67 (14th)
- AP Poll: 10th
- SRS: 21.59 (19th), SOS: 9.36 (63rd)
- Sports-Ref ORtg: 119.1 (27th), DRtg: 101.0 (51st)

**Tennessee (6-seed):**
- Record: 22-11 (11-7 SEC, 4th)
- KenPom: #15, AdjEM +26.06, AdjO 121.1 (35th), AdjD 95.0
- Torvik: #15, AdjOE 121.51 (33rd), AdjDE 95.48 (13th)
- AP Poll: 25th
- SRS: 22.08 (17th), SOS: 11.99 (18th)
- Sports-Ref ORtg: 116.8 (51st), DRtg: 101.9 (65th)

Key observations:
- The KenPom/Torvik ratings are remarkably close (13 vs 15, AdjEM 26.49 vs 26.06)
- Tennessee's defense is slightly better (AdjD 95.0 vs 95.7 KenPom)
- Virginia's offense is slightly better (AdjO 122.2 vs 121.1 KenPom)
- Tennessee played a much tougher schedule (SOS 18th vs 63rd; SRS 22.08 vs 21.59)
- Virginia has a far better record (29-4 vs 22-11)
- Virginia is the higher seed (3 vs 6)
- Tennessee had Nate Ament returning from injury with rest time
- Tennessee lost early in SEC Tournament (to Vanderbilt 75-68)
- Virginia was ACC runner-up with a 29-4 record

This is an extremely close matchup by the advanced metrics. Virginia has the better record and higher seed, while Tennessee played a tougher schedule. The efficiency margins are nearly identical. Virginia's 29-4 record and 3-seed status give them the edge in a near coin-flip.

  "pick": {
    "gameId": "r32-midwest-3",
    "winnerId": "virginia",
    "confidence": 0.57,
    "rationale": "This is an exceptionally close matchup between two elite defensive teams, with Virginia (KenPom #13, AdjEM +26.49) and Tennessee (KenPom #15, AdjEM +26.06) separated by less than half a point in adjusted efficiency margin and virtually identical defensive ratings (AdjD 95.7 vs 95.0). Virginia's decisive edge comes from its 29-4 record and 15-3 ACC mark as the conference runner-up, reflecting far greater consistency than Tennessee's 22-11 record that included an early SEC Tournament exit to Vanderbilt, while the Cavaliers' slight offensive advantage (AdjO 122.2 vs 121.1) could be the difference in what projects as a low-scoring, grind-it-out affair between two pack-line-style defenses. Tennessee's tougher schedule (SOS 18th vs 63rd) and the return of Nate Ament from injury give the Vols a credible path, but Virginia's superior record, higher seed, and home-court-like momentum from a dominant ACC season make them the narrow favorite."
  },
